title: Claude Skills Explained Simply (Master in 7 Minutes)
summary: Claude Skills enable users to create persistent custom instructions and automations that Claude remembers and reuses across conversations. The video demonstrates building a pizza shop catering quote skill that converts customer emails into branded PDF quotes, highlighting both practical applications and security considerations for using third-party skills.
writeup: A practical walkthrough of Claude Skills, the saved-instruction-set feature in the Claude desktop app that persists reusable workflows across sessions. The video explains the underlying file format (a YAML-front-matter Markdown file in a named folder), demonstrates the Skill Creator tool as a no-code authoring path, and works through a concrete catering-quote example. The through-line is a security warning: over a third of publicly available third-party skills carry security flaws, some designed to silently exfiltrate files through code execution.
key_points: A skill is a folder containing a skill.md file with YAML front matter and a Markdown body; Claude loads skills on demand, so 50 to 60 installed skills impose no meaningful slowdown.

The Skill Creator is a built-in skill that accepts a plain-language description, asks clarifying questions, and then generates and saves the correctly formatted skill file, removing the need to hand-author YAML.
Plugins (such as a marketing plugin) bundle multiple skills, agents, and connectors into a single install, delivering a full workflow rather than a single saved instruction set.

Studies cited in the video found that over a third of publicly available third-party AI skills contain security flaws; some are designed to instruct Claude to grab files and transmit them without the user's knowledge, exploiting the code-execution capability skills can invoke.

Anthropic-built and Skill-Creator-generated skills are vetted or self-authored and therefore safe; the guidance is to read any third-party skill file before installing it, or build custom ones rather than downloading them.
Skills work on all Claude plans (free, pro, max, enterprise) but require the code execution and file creation capability to be enabled in settings and a signed-in account.
